@sebastian-luna-valero I am trying the images and I just noticed that https://github.com/pangeo-data/geo-open-hack-2024/blob/main/docs/chunking_introduction.ipynb does not work anymore.

Do we still have access to the corresponding object storage? We started to use it since FOSS4F 2022:
```
fs = s3fs.S3FileSystem(anon=True,
      client_kwargs={
         'endpoint_url': 'https://object-store.cloud.muni.cz'
      })

is the proposed structure:

```bash
├── LICENSE
├── policy-acceptable-use.txt
├── providers
│   ├── kubernetes
│   │   ├── daskhub
│   │   │   ├── charts
│   │   │   ├── Chart.yaml
│   │   │   ├── templates
│   │   │   └── values.yaml
│   │   └── README.md
│   └── openstack
│       └── README.md
├── README.md
└── users
    ├── how-to
    │   ├── object-storage-openstack-cli.md
    │   ├── object-storage-openstack-horizon.md
    │   ├── SwiftXarrayTest.ipynb
    │   └── TestMinIO.ipynb
    └── users-getting-started.md
```

The idea is to separate out documentation for those who want to deploy the pangeo ecosystem in EOSC (`providers`) from those who want to use it (`users`).

For `providers` I also wanted to show the options we have covered:
* virtualised k8s deployment: `providers/openstack`
* baremetal k8s deployment: `providers/kubernetes`

For `providers/kubernetes` I would like to push instructions for both `DaskHub` and `BinderHub` with `Dask-Gateway`.

What do you ```bash
├── LICENSE
├── policy-acceptable-use.txt
├── providers
│   ├── kubernetes
│   │   ├── daskhub
│   │   │   ├── charts
│   │   │   ├── Chart.yaml
│   │   │   ├── templates
│   │   │   └── values.yaml
│   │   └── README.md
│   └── openstack
│       └── README.md
├── README.md
└── users
    ├── how-to
    │   ├── object-storage-openstack-cli.md
    │   ├── object-storage-openstack-horizon.md
    │   ├── SwiftXarrayTest.ipynb
    │   └── TestMinIO.ipynb
    └── users-getting-started.md
```

The idea is to separate out documentation for those who want to deploy the pangeo ecosystem in EOSC (`providers`) from those who want to use it (`users`).

For `providers` I also wanted to show the options we have covered:
* virtualised k8s deployment: `providers/openstack`
* baremetal k8s deployment: `providers/kubernetes`

For `providers/kubernetes` I would like to push instructions for both `DaskHub` and `BinderHub` with `Dask-Gateway`.

What do you
